## Notes

The issue is that it seems that in a Bayesian logistic regression, if you have a categorical variable with multiple levels (i.e. control, low dose, high dose) then the estimate of treatment effect in the high dose arm is influenced by what happens in the low dose arm.





## Example




::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
n <- 10
success_vector <- function(x, n) {
  c(
    rep(1,x),
    rep(0,n - x)

  )
}

d1 <- data.frame(
  id = seq(1, n*3),
  arm = rep(c("A", "B", "C"), each = n),
  outcome = 
    c(success_vector(5, n),
  success_vector(5, n), success_vector(8, n))



)

d2 <- data.frame(
  id = seq(1, n*3),
  arm = rep(c("A", "B", "C"), each = n),
  outcome = 
    c(success_vector(5, n),
  success_vector(8, n), success_vector(8, n))



)
```
:::




With GLM, you get the same estimate for dose C


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
glm(outcome ~ arm, data = d1, family = binomial) |> broom::tidy()
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
# A tibble: 3 × 5
  term         estimate std.error statistic p.value
  <chr>           <dbl>     <dbl>     <dbl>   <dbl>
1 (Intercept) -3.96e-16     0.632 -6.27e-16   1.00 
2 armB         4.52e-16     0.894  5.06e-16   1    
3 armC         1.39e+ 0     1.01   1.37e+ 0   0.171
```


:::

```{.r .cell-code}
glm(outcome ~ arm, data = d2, family = binomial) |> broom::tidy()
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
# A tibble: 3 × 5
  term        estimate std.error statistic p.value
  <chr>          <dbl>     <dbl>     <dbl>   <dbl>
1 (Intercept) 2.36e-16     0.632  3.73e-16   1    
2 armB        1.39e+ 0     1.01   1.37e+ 0   0.171
3 armC        1.39e+ 0     1.01   1.37e+ 0   0.171
```


:::
:::




However, with a Bayesian logistic regression model you get different estimates for armC



::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
model1 <- brm(outcome ~ arm,
data = d1,
family = bernoulli,
init = 0, seed = 20260622,
chains = 4, warmup = 1000, iter = 3500,
refresh = 0, silent = 2
)

model2 <- brm(outcome ~ arm,
data = d2,
family = bernoulli,
init = 0, seed = 20260622,
chains = 4, warmup = 1000, iter = 3500,
refresh = 0, silent = 2
)
```
:::





::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
broom.mixed::tidy(model1)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
# A tibble: 3 × 8
  effect component group term        estimate std.error conf.low conf.high
  <chr>  <chr>     <lgl> <chr>          <dbl>     <dbl>    <dbl>     <dbl>
1 fixed  cond      NA    (Intercept) -0.0145      0.655   -1.31       1.27
2 fixed  cond      NA    armB         0.00592     0.938   -1.84       1.83
3 fixed  cond      NA    armC         1.59        1.09    -0.425      3.87
```


:::

```{.r .cell-code}
broom.mixed::tidy(model2)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
# A tibble: 3 × 8
  effect component group term        estimate std.error conf.low conf.high
  <chr>  <chr>     <lgl> <chr>          <dbl>     <dbl>    <dbl>     <dbl>
1 fixed  cond      NA    (Intercept)  -0.0372     0.665   -1.36       1.29
2 fixed  cond      NA    armB          1.58       1.09    -0.429      3.89
3 fixed  cond      NA    armC          1.57       1.07    -0.411      3.80
```


:::
:::
